entry.title: Re: How-To Create a Better/(Worse) Variance Report with the 'New Model' in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@bluemustard 

Hi - unfortunately, I was not able to get that issue resolved.  The users ended up using the MS Excel add-on more often, anyways.  Sorry I didn't have a better response.

Regards

Dennis

entry.title: No data returned from table ANEP in HANA studio
entry.content: I'm currently having no data information on the table ANEP using FAAV_ANEP

entry.title: Re: Filter on multiple criteria
entry.content:
That makes sense. With the updated snippet I get: A number, currency amount, boolean, date, time, date-time, or string is expected here.

Then it shows this:

if IsNull ({@Filter})
then 0
else if {@Filter} = "Active or Under Renewal Process" or

then -3
else if {@Filter} = "Expired"
then -2
else 0

entry.title: Re: samlsinglesignon
entry.content: did you find an answer, I have the same problem. Also even though my user is authenticated by SSO, it is not from Hybris perspective.

entry.title: Re: What You Need to Know About Blogging Post-Migration
entry.content:
Hi, @javier_rp1:

We address that topic in this post: https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/addressing-common-questions-and-complaints-about-the-new-platform/ba-p/13598606 (section: Feeds by publication date (not just recent activity)). For now, we have created videos that might help you use search to find recently published blog posts: https://pages.community.sap.com/resources/how-to-videos.

Kind regards,

--Jerry

entry.title: Re: How-To Create a Better/(Worse) Variance Report with the 'New Model' in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
@dwilson13027 

Thank you for the quick response. 

The only workaround we found was to make another hierarchy and nest the new account (without the calculation) within the hierarchy.

Regards

Blue

entry.title: Re: Next Philly Cheesesteak, er, Stammtisch?
entry.content:
Hi, @JimSpath:

I'm willing to try anything! Keep us posted about your travel schedule, and we'll plan something for downtown Philly.

All the best,

--Jerry

entry.title: Re: Filter on multiple criteria
entry.content: The extra "or" on line 3 looks like the culprit.

entry.title: Re: HANA Change Management Strategies/Comparisons/Use cases
entry.content:
Hello Experts!
Could anyone guide me if SAP HANA Database changes are implemented/ migrated/ imported using Change and Transport System (CTS), will these changes appear under E070 table?

entry.title: Re: SLP_CTS: Error during deployment initialization: Not...
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

I understand this is very old post. However, if someone is looking for answer, here it is.

I too faced this issue and found that you need to ensure your subaccount should not hold any white space (or) if it has white spaces, you need to add a filler while configuring destination. PFB example. 

For ex : If you have your sub account name as "SAP Subaccount QA", you may end up with this issue. So, I created my QA subaccount as "SAPsubaccountQA" and configured my destination. This worked. 

However, if your subaccount has spaces - then ensure you have added "%20" for space as filler. For ex: If you have subaccount name as "SAP Subaccount QA", then while configuring your destination, configure it as "SAP%20Subaccount%20QA". The overall config URL should look like this - "
https://deploy-service.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/slprot/SAP%20Subaccount%20QA/qa/slp "

PFB SAP Documentation link of this. 
https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-transport-management/sap-cloud-transport-management/creating-destinations-using-sap-cloud-deployment-service-with-basic-authentication?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Deprecation of Classic Reporting
entry.content: Is there somewhere we can get a screenshot of where the Archive & Print function will be in the future?

entry.title: Re: Fatal error during deployment of CPI transport reques...
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

I understand this is very old post. However, if someone is looking for answer, here it is.

I too faced this issue and found that you need to ensure your subaccount should not hold any white space (or) if it has white spaces, you need to add a filler while configuring destination. PFB example. 

For ex : If you have your sub account name as "SAP Subaccount QA", you may end up with this issue. So, I created my QA subaccount as "SAPsubaccountQA" and configured my destination. This worked. 

However, if your subaccount has spaces - then ensure you have added "%20" for space as filler. For ex: If you have subaccount name as "SAP Subaccount QA", then while configuring your destination, configure it as "SAP%20Subaccount%20QA". The overall config URL should look like this - "
https://deploy-service.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/slprot/SAP%20Subaccount%20QA/qa/slp "

PFB SAP Documentation link of this. 

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-transport-management/sap-cloud-transport-management/creating-destinations-using-sap-cloud-deployment-service-with-basic-authentication?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: Controlling the Visibility of SAP Enable Now and SAP Companion Content
entry.content:
Hi Karen,

There are two options to do this:

1. User Role details can be passed through the Single Sign On (SSO) process so users automatically have the relevant User and therefore Business Role when the user accesses Enable Now.  The corporategroups attribute is used for this purpose.  You can learn more about this through this KBA, please discuss this option with the person who sets up SSO in your organization, to see if this is an option

2. User details can be loaded and updated through a spreadsheet load.  The easiest way to manage this process is:
 - Open Manager
- Select Administration > Users
- Click the Export button and export the user details
- Update the Position value for each user to define the User/Business Roles relevant for that user; roles are separated by commas.  In the image below, the corresponding user will have two User/Business Roles, Reviewers and Learners
- In Manager, select Administration > User Import and import the modified user details

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Purchase document is allocated to asset in SAP Public...
entry.content:
Hi @imel_99 

I could find no SSCUI / Configuration activity in S/4HANA Cloud to change the error to a warning like mentioned in the thread.

 

For S/4HANA Cloud there is a KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3154459 about this error 

The Best Practice is to use an Investment Project to post the costs and then settle the project to an assets (See Best Practice Guides Project Control - Finance (‏1NT‏) and Period-End Closing - Maintenance Orders (‏BF7‏)).

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: Filter on multiple criteria
entry.content: I see it but I didn't write that. That is what Crystal presented to me. This tells me something in the original formula is not acceptable by Crystal is my guess.

entry.title: Re: Digital Discovery Assessment - The guided decision between SAP S/4HANA Public or Private Cloud E

where can i find some example of DDA's final report ?  in order to known sections and parts of the document

entry.title: EWM Putaway Storage Section Indicator Defaulting to Incorrect Putaway Bin
entry.content: Product does not have a Fixed Bin assigned and the Putaway Storage Section Indicator is set to HTZN in EWM Product Master Data, then the assumption is the automatic warehouse task Storage Bin for Putaway should go to a Bin with the Storage Section HTZN, it does not.

entry.title: Re: Error in Loading Learning Center
entry.content:
Hi Adeola,

The values of the DATA_URL_WPB2 and LEARNING_APP_BACKEND_URL are not exactly the same.  The following example shows the difference:

DATA_URL_WPB2 example
https://customerABC.enable-now.cloud.sap/wa/workareaID/

LEARNING_APP_BACKEND_URL
https://customerABC.enable-now.cloud.sap/

The LEARNING_APP_BACKEND_URL must be combined with the LEARNING_APP_WORKSPACE parameter that would be set to the ID of the Enable Now Workarea containing the learning content; in my example, it would be set to workareaID.

In  my example, you must replace customerABC with your Enable Now system name and workareaID with the actual ID of your Workarea.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Query regarding SAP Inside Track - Hyderabad 2024
entry.content:
We want to do big this time so planning in progress once dates are final will start publishing. 

keep an watch on Hyd community page

entry.title: EWM blind cycle count by bin (not product and/or batch)
entry.content:
We're struggling with a bit of an issue right now with EWM.  We're getting feedback that we need to develop a custom solution for this.  But it just seems very odd to me that there is no way to do this out of the box or via config.  It seems like a very common need - "WMS 101" to me.  Any thoughts?

**Business Scenario**

Bin B01-01-01 has a physical pallet that has 40 batches of material X on it.   A user did a bin-to-bin move for material X,  batch 1234 for the full quantity of 10 to a new bin but never physically moved the stock.   Two days later, material X is cycle counted in bin B01-01-01.    Note: All batches are barcoded and pointing outward for scanning on all four sides of the physical pallet.

During the cycle count by WO, the user is directed to count material X, for a specific batch presented by the system.  The order of batches being presented causes the user to walk around the pallet searching for each batch to be scanned many times.  This is causing inefficiencies with counting a material that is batch managed when multiple batches exist in the bin / on a physical pallet.  

During an audit of this bin, it was found that the user missed counting the extra batch that was on the pallet as this batch 1234 was bin to bin moved in the system only, not physically moved.  According to the system the cycle count document was 100% counted, no differences.    The user explained that they are moving around the pallet so many times they cannot keep track of what batches are scanned and what batches are not.   A more efficient order of scanning batches is required for counting batch managed items within a bin / on a pallet.

**Requested Solution**

In this business case the user requires a more efficient process of scanning the batches for the batch managed material being counted within the bin / on a physical pallet.   The user needs the ability to scan the batches in the physical order found on the pallet.     Example:  40 batches on a pallet, since all barcodes are pointing outward 10 batch barcodes displayed on each side of the pallet.  This would allow the user to start on one side scanning from the top to bottom in physical order, entering each count after scanning each batch.   If the batch is not found on the inventory document, but is a valid batch in the system, the user should receive a message batch not found on inventory document.  Add batch for material X? Yes or No.   If yes the user will enter the count and continue to scan the remaining batches.

entry.title: Re: Filter on multiple criteria
entry.content: Try putting {@Filter} on your report in the Details section and see what the output is. If it gives you an error right away, then post your current {@Filter} formula plus a screenshot of what's going on in the Formula Editor...i.e. the top left where is shows the values and the point of failure.

entry.title: Re: Start Hour of Sale at # instead of 1
entry.content:
You could create a variable something like...

=If [Hour of Sale] = "12am" Then 0
ElseIf [Hour of Sale] = "1am" Then 1
...
ElseIf [Hour of Sale = "10pm" Then 22
Else 23

and sort by that.

Or put a custom sort on your [Hour of Sale] column.

entry.title: Re: Why Is My Content Marked as Spam?
entry.content:
F*@#$)**@#$ spam 'filter' !!!

Just spent an hour composing an answer and because of the finicky formatting features I had to edit/repost a few times and ... *BAM* ... "this reply has been flagged and removed as spam".

How about updating the spam 'filter' to take into consideration the history of the person posting the message/reply/answer?

And the cherry on top ... a few minutes later I'm awarded the 'Contributor' badge (for posting spam).

... crossing fingers and hoping this comment isn't flagged as spam ...

entry.title: How to the find the usage of a Fiori app from the backend?
entry.content: How to find the usage of a Fiori app from the backend? The metric interested is how many users are accessing the app on a daily basis for a selected period of time. Any suggestions will be highly appreciated.

entry.title: Where can I find learning on the Termination Workbench
entry.content: Hi, I am keen to learn how the Termination Workbench sits together. How is it set up how does it know what to look for when commencing the pay component of the cycle.  Cheers Katja

entry.title: SAP Fiori Elements - Save Variant/Layout not working (and want to remove unnecessary column)
entry.content:
I have created an SAP Fiori Elements app. At first the list report would not even let me resize the columns (and they were very badly spaced out) but eventually I discovered that it would let me resize columns if I change the table to "Grid Table".

I am having two issues on the list report page:

1. When I resize the columns it allows me to save the layout but when I refresh, the layout I saved disappears.

2. There is an empty column on the left of the table that the user does not want as it takes up much needed space.

Any advice on these issues would be really appreciated.

The empty column on the left needs to be removed:

This is what I do to save the layout:

The new layout is saved:

But when I click on the drop down arrow:

And when I refresh, it is gone:

entry.title: Re: Take Photo in Maintenance Apps on Microsoft Surface G...
entry.content:
Hi Enda

Thank you for responding. I find this really interesting that the Microsoft devices are not supported when SAP and Microsoft are in partnership and working so closely together - https://www.sap.com/partners/find/microsoft.html

The issue with the Apple and Android devices is they are a single user sign on, so they do not support SSO for multiple users which is the method for logging into S4HC.. 

Rather disappointing when you want to utilise Mobility and are restricted to technology that doesnt align to business usage.

Thanks

Higgo

entry.title: Contar lineas de registros en documento de marketing antes de guardar
entry.content:
Saludos, alguien sabe como retornar la cantidad de registros de un documento de marketing antes de guardar el documento para someterno a un modelo de aprobación?

 

Agradecido de antemano

entry.title: Re: Enter data on customizing system

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hello @David_Chaviano 

As 40+ working in quality, I'm in the category .

As a professional aged 40+ with experience in quality management, I fall into the category of People looking to switch their careers mid-life.

After numerous years in the field of quality management, I have developed a strong passion for IT solutions, process automation, and enhancing the efficiency of workplace operations for my colleagues. As a result, I am actively seeking new opportunities in these areas.

I have recently completed training in Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, and I am currently pursuing training in Signavio.

I would sincerely appreciate the opportunity to participate in your program and contribute my skills and expertise.

entry.title: Re: Enter data on customizing system
entry.content: On customizing system because I modify the company address and logo in standard PO layout and I want to enter transaction data to show the PO layout after modified the po header and footer

entry.title: Re: Enter data on customizing system
entry.content: Is it applicable on customizing system or should enter transaction data on test system only

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

My name is John and I'm currently working as Data Processor here in the Philippines.

I identify myself as: 

  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

Most of the companies here in the Philippines are using SAP technologies that's why I decided to participate in this initiative to learn more about SAP technologies such as SAP HANA Cloud and ABAP Programming.

I hope my application will be consider for a free certification attempt. This will be a great help for me as I'm looking forward to make a career shift into SAP.

 

Thanks,

John

entry.title: Re: Enter data on customizing system
entry.content:
You can definitely create transactions like Purchase Orders in the DEV system as you describe to for example test the output and other system behaviour. For a test strategy (ie. what to test when and in which system) I recommend that you check the task "Create Test Strategy for SAP S/4HANA Cloud" in the Activate Roadmap Viewer.

Hope this helps.

Esther

entry.title: Re: Enter data on customizing system
entry.content: Not dev system my question was on customizing system also the modification in layouts like adding logo is key user extensibility please could you clear your answer regarding customizing system and key user extensibility thanks in advance

entry.title: SAP 分析云 2024.06 版新功能抢先看
entry.content:
SAP 分析云是一款先进的商务分析云解决方案，集商业智能 (BI)、增强分析、预测分析和企业规划功能于一体，消除单点解决方案和据孤岛挑战，能提供您需要的全面云分析功能。

本文介绍了 SAP 分析云 2024.06 版本的新功能。这些新功能已经在 SAP 分析云 FastTrack 客户的系统上线。对于 SAP 分析云季度发布周期 (QRC) 客户，此版本及其功能将作为 QRC 2024 年第2季度版本的一部分提供。这里可以查看 QRC 发布时间表。

 

系统和技术要求

尽管不是必需的，我们推荐您升级到最新的 SAP 分析云代理版本 1.0.399，利用所有数据采集类型。

除以下情况外，您的代理需要1.0.75或以上版本：

  • SAP ERP/SQL 数据库版本1.0.99
  • SAP Universe (UNX)版本1.0.365（ SAP分析云代理需要额外的设置步骤，有关详细信息，请参阅 SAP 说明3262098）
  • SAP Business Warehouse (BW)版本1.0.353
  • SAP HANA View版本1.0.235
  • File Server版本1.0.248

更多详细信息，请参阅系统要求和技术先决条件。

有关将 SAP BW 与 SAP 分析云集成的受支持功能和所需更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2541557 以了解更多详细信息。

有关通过 SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect 与 SAP 分析云来实时访问 Universe 时的受支持功能和所需更新，请参阅 SAP Note 2771921 以了解更多详细信息。

请从“Service Marketplace”下载最新版本的“简易部署工具箱”。

  • 为避免下载旧版本并可能让用户面对 Cloud Connector 与 SAPJVM 或 Apache Tomcat 的绑定不当带来的漏洞，只有三个最新版本可供下载。

关于云代理的版本变更管理细节，请参考 SAP Note 3264839。
 

更新亮点

  • 数据发掘
    • 数据分析器中支持工作区 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 导出为 PDF 时设置适合页面宽度 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 数据建模
    • 提供“单位”属性类型 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 启用分析引擎的优化方案编写器
  • 企业计划
    • 数据操作 - 新增了日历用法的显示
    • 计划触发器－更新了格式设置选项  |（SAP路线图浏览器）
  • 故事设计
    • 在优化故事体验中替换模型 |（SAP路线图浏览器）
    • 从垂直筛选器面板中访问故事筛选器和页面筛选器
    • 支持基于SAP BW 二级结构的瀑布图
    • 向统计图的标题和页脚中添加超链接

 

数据发掘

数据分析器中支持工作区

现在使用数据分析器时，可以从工作区打开模型，也可以将洞察保存到工作区并从工作区重新打开。如果一个故事使用的模型保存到了工作区，用户也可以从它的统计图或表中打开相应的数据分析器了。（SAP路线图浏览器）

 

导出为 PDF 时设置适合页面宽度

数据分析器中将数据导出为 PDF 文件时，我们增加了一个“适合页面宽度”选项。用户可以将表的大小调整为 PDF 导出的实际页面宽度。（SAP路线图浏览器）

 

数据建模

提供“单位”属性类型

我们新增了一个属性类型：“单位”，可用于通用类型和组织类型的维。该属性的最大长度为127个字符，可以在“度量”部分进行设置，以显示数据的计量单位。

就像货币一样，单位属性可以添加到维。在度量处确定好单位属性后，MDS 可以确保不会在不同单位之间进行聚合操作（防止结果错误）。（SAP路线图浏览器）

 

启用分析引擎的优化方案编写器

SAP 分析云2024.06以后的版本中的所有模型，都将默认使用新的查询处理引擎——“分析引擎的优化场景编写器”。这是将全球数据中心迁移至SAP HANA Cloud Platform 的一部分。此引擎将在系统更新期间自动推出。如果有紧急情况需要，只要租户尚未迁移至 SAP HANA Cloud Platform，引擎就可以恢复到旧引擎。

有关详细信息，请参阅分析引擎的优化方案编写器。

 

企业计划

数据操作 - 新增了日历用法的显示

在“数据操作设置”中，用户可以查看数据操作在其他操作或应用（例如故事、多步操作或分析应用中）的使用位置。此版本中，“使用位置”部分新包含了处于未完成状态的日历任务。此外，只有单个任务和任务系列支持此操作，任务序列的单一任务不支持。

 

计划触发器－更新了格式设置选项

现在可以在“主题首选项”对话框中修改计划触发器的默认样式。此外，计划触发器现在支持矢量格式（如 SVG）的使用。在每个计划触发器的样式设置面板中，用户可以将样式设置恢复为默认首选项，也可以将触发器按钮或字体样式分别重置为默认值。

这样使用户可以更集中地管理默认样式，包含管理计划触发器的外观。（SAP路线图浏览器）

 

 

故事设计

在优化故事体验中替换模型

此版本支持用户在优化故事体验中统一替换模型。这个功能允许基于单个模型的替换，且要求替换模型必须和原始模型具有相同的数据源类型、相同数量和类型的结构维。原始模型和替换模型不需要共享相同的连接，也不需要具有相同数量的对象（账户、度量、维或属性）。但是，针对实时模型，替换模型背后的源系统的版本必须与原始模型的源系统版本相同或比它更高。（SAP路线图浏览器）

 

有些数据模型只能用作原始模型，不能用作替换模型。以下是一些原始模型和对应允许的替换模型的示例：

  • SAP Datasphere 中的分析数据集可以替换为 SAP Datasphere 中的分析模型
  • 经典账户模型可以替换为“含账户维的新模型类型”
  • 公用数据集可以替换为不含账户维的新模型类型

阅读由 Tunir Kapil 撰写的博文，了解更多信息。

 

从垂直筛选器面板中访问故事筛选器和页面筛选器

用户现在可以在垂直筛选器栏中查看故事和页面过滤器，并与之交互。在当前页面上的每个输入控件中，垂直过滤器面板上新增的“应用于此页面”部分将显示页面过滤器。用户导航到其他页面时，面板将相应更新。

请注意，这仅在垂直过滤器面板中可用。

 

支持基于 SAP BW 二级结构的瀑布图

我们现在支持基于 SAP BW 模型二级结构绘制瀑布图。查看数据必须满足以下条件：

  • 如果不存在分层度量的情况，对二级结构使用单个度量和单值筛选器后，图中只会显示单一条形。
  • 在二级结构使用多个度量和单值筛选器，将导致每个度量都是一个条形，且显示的数据基于二级结构筛选器。
  • 使用单个度量和多个二级结构成员将导致次级结构的每个成员都递增或减少，其中最后一个节点显示总计。

此功能的限制：

  • 无法更改辅助结构成员的顺序。
  • 如果至少有一个层次结构节点，展开/折叠功能将无法使用。

 

向统计图的标题和页脚中添加超链接

对于已有的从经典体验转换而来的故事，我们保留了其统计图标题和页脚中的超链接。

我们新增了在统计图标题和页脚中定义多个超链接的选项（与文本微件相同）。

现在还可以通过页面和故事超链接传递外部过滤器的信息。但是，统计图标题和页脚的设置中，“仅应用选定维”选项是隐藏的。

请注意，为了支持超链接功能，动态的图标标题会转换为静态标题。

附加资源：

  • 如何启用优化体验
  • 优化体验限制

 


本文仅提供 SAP 分析云的最新功能和增强功能的高级概述。您可以在针对FastTrack客户的应用内帮助指南中找到有关这些功能的更多详细信息，如果您想了解更多关于FastTrack和季度发布周期发布的信息，请参阅这篇SAP知识库文章。

entry.title: Re: Why missing milestone button
entry.content: This is close after configuration

entry.title: Re: Quote 2.0 : Displaying a message to the User in a spe...

entry.title: Re: Schedule Line Category determine by header block
entry.content: Closed

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA alternative BOM in CU50
entry.content:
Hi,

As I notice that when we have the alternative BOM for the KMAT, the CU50 can't display BOM, meaning BOM doesn't be exposed even we have the production version.

So do you have any comment here? If KMAT have alternative BOM, how to select in CU50?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: The requests received by the Report Processing Server...
entry.content: i am using both latest versions of Sap Designer & crystal reports, i did get a reply for SAP which is below stated "SAP Crystal Reports 2020 reports are not supported in REST SDK as of now (e.g., 4.3 SP04)" they then asked "Can you please confirm if you can use Open document for the same workflow?" so ive made some new code and i can form a URL and generate a report via the opendoc link but i cant seem to download the PDF directly regardless of the output option in the URL

entry.title: HANA 2.0 SPS 07 long running threads
entry.content:
We have recently upgrade from HANA 2.0 SPS 05 to SPS 07 (rev 76) and have noticed that threads for the PreprocessorPool and WebDispatcher-Main-Thread run continually from startup. These threads do not appear in SP5 version and trying to work out if this is standard in the latest release.

I have also looked at my Cockpit Server, which is running on rev 73 and it has the same thing occurring.

Thanks

David

entry.title: How to mass add technical object description in a language other than English
entry.content:
When login system in a language other than English, the description missing, how to mass add technical object description in a language other than English?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to mass add technical object description in a lan...
entry.content:
Hi 

Batch request for equipment and functional location could mass add or change technical object description in a language other than English. Please note during using Postman, it is necessary to add "sap-language" in "Headres", you could select the language what you need.

For batch request please see below link

Equipment :Batch Request

Functional location : Batch Request

best regards

icey

entry.title: How to dynamically pass parameters to SimbaDSN in code
entry.content: How to dynamically pass parameters to SimbaDSN in code ,For example, passing "key file path" as a dynamic parameter?

entry.title: Re: IntelliJ for SAP CAP
entry.content: Found this plugin: https://github.tools.sap/cap/intellij-cds

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Integration scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system
entry.content: good information

entry.title: Re: RISE with SAP: ‘Defense in Depth’ Security Architecture with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition
entry.content:
Hi Jana,

As you mentioned above regarding admin access,

"Secure Administration Access:

  • SAP Cloud Administrator is allowed from whitelisted IP address and Multi-Factor Authentications is required."

    Here SAP Cloud Administrator refers to SAP Administrator (say SAP Basis technical) or Customer Administrator (say SAP Basis Application). If it refers to SAP Administrator (say SAP Basis technical) then where the IP whitelists are maintained and is it auditable? can SAP Administrator access be restricted with Customer Azure AD MFA if not where is the MFA is maintained and can it be audited?

    Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP Ariba Integration scenarios with SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system
entry.content: Hello! Are there any documented limitations on extensions on the 42K scenario for integration of additional fields in purchase order or invoice in public cloud? We are looking specifically for some custom fields required particulary in invoices for tax localization requirements. We are also interested in customizations for Ariba Procurement (Buying). Can anyone share? Ariba Network Integration for SAP Business Suite SAP Integration Suite SAP Ariba Procurement, cloud edition  SAP Business Network for Procurement and SAP Business Network for Supply Chain

entry.title: Object Assignment to Real Estate Contract using Real Estate Object from C4RE
entry.content: I am having problem with assigning Real Estate Object to a contract from C4RE. When REO object type is being selected, only the number ranges from Group Enable Use and Group for occupancy is shown on the selection. Individual RE objects are not being shown. Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Query Regarding Tomorrow's MCQ Test
entry.content: They might mail us and should update us,or else we have to keep things aside and wait for link to crop up in  mail or site.

entry.title: Correct amortization passed mistakenly on an auc
entry.content:
Hello fellows,

An accountant made mistakenly an amortization plan for an asset under construction auc. This led to an error in AFAB. An amortization passed on the auc which is not configured for. Now when we run afab with restart, it returns error AU133.

Is there a way to undo that amortization or is there any other solution?

Cordially,

Aziz

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Kolkata 2024
entry.content: @DataTherapist maam our team has not yet received the confirmation mail or mcq round 1 quiz link for sit track kolkata sit icoe hackathon

entry.title: Re: SAC Currency Conversion Rate from previous Month (Pri...
entry.content:
I can think of two options(not ideal of course)

  1. Create a conversion measure with booking date + 1（month), but this only work when there is one month shift

     

     

     

  2.  Create a base measure to first load previous month value and then do currency conversion

entry.title: Option for user to select versions in waterfall chart
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have created a waterfall chart in SAC story and want to provide the option for users to select the versions as they want.

While creating an input control using filter by range, there is no option to select all the version.

Is there a way I can give users option to select versions in waterfall charts.

regards

Vish

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Kolkata 2024
entry.content: Our team has also not yet received any mail for round 1.

entry.title: Re: UI BubbleEvent property
entry.content:
Hi @ANKIT_CHAUHAN ,

No particular thing to achieve, just wondering if this is a bug or by design. 
AFAIK, the BubbleEvent property should prevent the system event from bubbling up to the next event.
For example, in a button BeforeItemPressed event, if I set the BubbleEvent = false, the UI API will not raise the AfterItemPressed event.

However, in a grid BeforeValidate event, even when setting the BubbleEvent = false, UI API still raise the AfterValidate event.

Below is the code i use to test 

    internal class Program
    {
        static SAPbouiCOM.Application app;
        static string formUID, grid1UID, grid2UID;


        [STAThread]
        static void Main()
        {
            try
            {
                SAPbouiCOM.SboGuiApi oGUI = new SAPbouiCOM.SboGuiApi();
                oGUI.Connect("0030002C0030002C00530041005000420044005F00440061007400650076002C0050004C006F006D0056004900490056");
                app = oGUI.GetApplication();
                app.ItemEvent += App_ItemEvent;

                //Create 1 form with 1 grid
                var fcp = app.CreateObject(SAPbouiCOM.BoCreatableObjectType.cot_FormCreationParams) as SAPbouiCOM.FormCreationParams;
                string sql1 = "SELECT CAST('A' AS NVARCHAR(1))  InvDesc, '1' LineIdx, '0' LineNum UNION ALL SELECT 'A', '2', '1' UNION ALL SELECT 'A', '1', '2' UNION ALL SELECT 'A', '2', '2'";

                var newForm = app.Forms.AddEx(fcp);
                formUID = newForm.UniqueID;
                newForm.Title = "Test Grid Validate";
                newForm.Width = 650;
                newForm.Height = 750;
                var dt = newForm.DataSources.DataTables.Add($"dt1");
                dt.ExecuteQuery(sql1);
                var grid = newForm.Items.Add($"grd1", SAPbouiCOM.BoFormItemTypes.it_GRID).Specific as SAPbouiCOM.Grid;
                grid.Item.Top = 10;
                grid.Item.Left = 10;
                grid.Item.Width = 600;
                grid.Item.Height = 300;
                grid.DataTable = dt;

                newForm.Visible = true;

                System.Windows.Forms.Application.Run();
            }
            catch (Exception Ex)
            {
                MessageBox.Show(Ex.Message);
            }
        }

        private static void App_ItemEvent(string FormUID, ref SAPbouiCOM.ItemEvent pVal, out bool BubbleEvent)
        {
            BubbleEvent = true;
            try
            {
                if(FormUID == formUID && pVal.ItemUID == "grd1" && pVal.ColUID=="LineNum" && pVal.EventType== BoEventTypes.et_VALIDATE && pVal.BeforeAction)
                {
                    //Before Action
                    app.StatusBar.SetText("Before Action triggered");
                    BubbleEvent = false;
                    return; 
                }
                if (FormUID == formUID && pVal.ItemUID == "grd1" && pVal.ColUID == "LineNum" && pVal.EventType == BoEventTypes.et_VALIDATE && !pVal.BeforeAction)
                {
                    //After Action
                    app.StatusBar.SetText("After Action triggered");
                }

            }
            catch (Exception Ex)
            {
                BubbleEvent = false;
                MessageBox.Show(Ex.Message);
            }
        }
    }

entry.title: Re: GraphQL in SAP Asset Performance Management
entry.content: Excellent brief on why this decision was made, great you published it so we weren't quessing.

We don't have such flexibility to maintain multiple values as Transit plant in V_T001_TRNSTPLNT. It will allow only one Plant = 1 company code.

 

Regards,

Karthik.

entry.title: CPQ often requires user to change password
entry.content:
According to user's feedback, he was required to change password 3 times within 3 months, can you please explain why user's password needs to be changed so frequently? Is it possible to set the password to be permanent.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: CPQ often requires user to change password
entry.content:
System raise password reset request based on the password expiration date which you can see it under user's page.

For more details refer this following link: Create a User | SAP Help Portal

If you wish to have permanent password, leave that field empty.

entry.title: Re: Transfer of Concurrent users to named users
entry.content: You can upload a csv to user management by mapping the right property for is_concurrent.

entry.title: regarding hana registeration to solman
entry.content:
I want to use hdmlcm, I think we need to make sure the route solman -> customer Hana.
How do I secure the route in this case?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: regarding hana registeration to solman
entry.content:
If you want to use hdblcm to register hana db to solman LMDB, please refer:
https://gad5158842f.us2.hana.ondemand.com/dtp/viewer/#/tree/3334/actions/52838:52846:52863/?version=current

entry.title: Leased assets
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have requirement to track leased assets when they are a part of the actual cost of the complete value of the assets. For example: A machine for $160,000 before tax and freight is being financed on a 2 yr. lease.  Rest of the project is for $180,000 which included the electrical and installation costs. The total project approved for $340,000.

Can I create a new asset class to track this? Should this feed into depreciation or amortization?

What happens when we keep the asset as the asset classes can’t be changed.

How will this affect the Capex process?

entry.title: Assembly Processing (SD-PS) on Public cloud. Any best practices available?
entry.content:
Hi Fellow Sapiens,

Is there any test script available for assembly processing (SD-PS) scenario on Public cloud. If not is there any alternative solution for it.

Kindly let me know.

Thanks,

Vinita.

entry.title: airlinestravel
entry.content: Are you going to be traveling by air to a distant location for your next trip? You're at the correct spot if your answer is in the affirmative. See the AirportsTerminalGuide for comprehensive details about every airport terminal worldwide.

entry.title: Re: Manage work center capacity for maintenance order
entry.content:
Hi Dany,

The "Manage Work Center Capacity" app is for a Production Engineer (PP) and as far as I am aware it can't bring in the Maintenance Work Centers (no amount of configuration will change that).

Do you have anyone on your project that is looking at Reporting/Analytics? You may need their help here, but If all you want to do is display Work Center capacity you might have an option there to look at the available CDS Views to see what is available for WC Capacities, then turn those into an Analytical Query, then create a tile for it and provision to the business. - it would then be a self-service style report.

That functionality is in the business role for Analytics Specialist, but it would be similar to this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-create-analytical-query-from-custom-cds-view/ba-p/13526834

Tim

entry.title: Re: Sap Inside Track Bangalore 17th Feb 2024

Very detailed blog!

Would you happen to know how I can get the Session ppt links for the other sessions where I had attended? They told me that ppts would be available but unfortunately i don't know where to obtain them.

entry.title: Re: Sap Inside Track Bangalore 17th Feb 2024

entry.title: Single Sign On to SAP Cloud Integration (CPI runtime) from an external Identity Provider
entry.content:
Introduction:

Yes, you read it right (and you read it right here !). There is an out-of-the-box approach to achieving a single sign-on (SSO) experience for user flows between a corporate identity provider (that authenticates and authorizes the user) and a tenant of Cloud Integration runtime (loosely called CPI worker) fully within the BTP ecosystem.

Ok, let’s zoom out a bit and break this down.

If you are reading this blog post, you probably know already that SAP BTP Services can leverage the OpenID Connect federation-based mechanics of SAP Cloud Identity Service (read: SAP IAS) to connect users from corporate Identity Providers like Entra ID (formerly known as Azure AD), Okta, etc. to XSUAA BTP’s OAuth Authorization Server.
This is certainly not uncharted and I did a detailed blog post a few months ago demonstrating this setup.

However, this setup applied mostly to browser-based SaaS applications (read: Design Time applications with a web frontend), and that brings us to the objective of this blog -> Customers want to put together a similar setup for their client applications that interface with SAP Cloud Integration’s IFLows (in other words, the CPI runtime).
Certainly, this is not impossible to achieve and solution blueprints like these have existed in the past:

  • My colleague Francisco’s blog puts API Management in between a client and Cloud Integration and enforces API Management to perform an OAuthSAMLBearer handshake.
  • Microsoft champion Martin Raepple teaches how to set up SAML Trust between Entra ID Identity Provider and BTP to set up a user impersonation flow.

However, these approaches were often seen as cumbersome to set up / troubleshoot and certainly not for the faint-hearted!

Solution Summary:

An easier solution can be described in two phrases: 'OpenID Connect' and 'Authorization Code grant type'. If you are super-smart then you've figured it out already. You can stop reading this blog and hack this yourself.
I wish you a nice day ahead! If you are like me and need a bit more explanation, keep reading 🙂

Here is the solution blueprint that explains that handshake:

SCENARIO: Flows that require end-user authentication from external Identity Providers can natively do so with OIDC and Authorization Code grant type

Step 0: Generate Service Instance / Service Key SAP Cloud Integration Runtime. Refer to this link. Instead of Client Credentials make sure to select  Authorization Code.

Step 1: Onboard the needed corporate identity providers in SAP IAS and set up the 'Application' that connects back to your SAP BTP Subaccount as a Trusted Identity Provider via OpenID Connect. Refer to my previous blog post for a detailed procedure. 

Step 2: Client (end-user)  initiates a connection to the required IFlow (or API artifact). This kicks off the 3-legged OAuth user login flow.

Step 3: As the user is not signed in, she is redirected to XSUAA's login endpoint, and upon login the IAS tenant's  OAuth server authorization endpoint at https://<IAS  tenant name>.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/authorize is invoked using the authorization code grant type. The details of the actual federation as part of the handshake have been omitted here for simplicity. But suffice it to say that the authorization code from the identity provider is made available to the IAS's callback endpoint and finally made available to XSUAA's authorize endpoint and exchanged for the actual access token. This access token will bear the user's scopes and role permissions needed to access the Cloud Integration's IFlow resource. 

Step 4: Once successfully authorized, on the receiver side of the IFlow, we will establish connections to 3 different types of backends for illustration purposes. a) S/4HANA Onpremise system over Cloud Connector and Principal Propagation b) SuccessFactors and c) S/4HANA Cloud with OAuth2 SAMLBearer Assertion security material. 

Putting it all together:

Let's get our hands dirty by putting together the sequence now. The prerequisites to follow along are listed below:

  • Administrator privileges in the BTP subaccount where the Integration Suite subscription exists.
  • An IAS Tenant (with Administrator privileges) that can be coupled (read: Trusted) with the said BTP Subaccount.
  • Privileges to create Applications (read: IDP configurations) in Entra ID (Azure AD) and/or Okta.
  • Postman Client.
  • Backend systems to which the frontend user principal can be propagated to. Either of S/4HANA OnPrem, S/4HANA Cloud, or SuccessFactors tenant.

Step 0: Create a Service Instance for the Authorization Code grant type

Create an instance of the 'Process Integration Runtime' Service (integration-flow service plan)  specifically with the authorization code grant type. You can copy the JSON snippet pasted below. Do not worry about the location of the redirect_uri. (When we get down to testing the flow, the browser will invoke the redirect_uri, but this has no consequence as the 'code' will be available for us to copy as a query parameter from the URL itself. When we test this from Postman the client, Postman does not invoke the URL. If you are curious to know, you can read about it here.) Also, make a note that we have specified refresh_token as part of the requested grant type. This will let us demonstrate the ability for clients to refresh the access token post-expiry. 

 

{
    "grant-types": [
        "refresh_token",
        "authorization_code"
    ],
    "redirect-uris": [
        "http://localhost"
    ],
    "roles": [
        "ESBMessaging.send"
    ]
}

 

With the service instance created, generate a service key (example block is pasted below). Grab the clientid, clientsecret, authorizationurl, tokenurl attributes. We will need these later.

 

Step 1: Configure OpenID Connect based Trusted Identity Provider of SAP IAS in your SAP BTP subaccount

This step is the heart-and-soul of our approach. We will couple an SAP IAS tenant with our BTP subaccount that has the subscription of our SAP Cloud Integration (SAP Integration Suite) tenant using OpenID Connect protocol and then onboard the desired external Identity Providers (I will demonstrate Entra ID and Okta) as corporate identity providers in the IAS administration console.
Since I've documented the steps in my previous blog, I will not repeat the exact steps here. Please refer to the following sections in the linked blog.

 

ObjectiveSteps to follow from the linked blog
Couple your BTP subaccount and your SAP IAS tenant.Steps 1-6
Configure applications (relying party) in Azure AD and Okta IDP based on OpenID Connect and SAP IAS as the callback URI.Steps 7 - 25
Configure application in Okta with SAML Trust to SAP IAS.Steps 26 - 33
Onboard the above Corporate Identity Provider configurations into SAP IAS.
Steps 34 - 46
configure IAS as the proxy Identity Provider and SAP BTP as the Service Provider.Steps 47 - 52

Nevertheless, here is a summary of the main steps involved in the setup.

1. The subaccount where the Integration Suite subscription exists has a 'Trusted connection' with the OpenID Connect protocol (not SAML) to the IAS tenant.

2. The IAS tenant has a 'Corporate Identity provider' connection to Azure AD (Entra ID) via a set of Application credentials and OpenID Connect protocol.

 

3. Notice the 'Application' settings on the Azure side. The redirect URI has been set to the IAS tenant's '../oath2/callback' segment

4. The IAS tenant has a 'Corporate Identity provider' connection to Okta IDP via a set of Application credentials and OpenID Connect protocol.

 

5. Notice the 'Application' settings on the Okta side. The redirect URI has been set to the IAS tenant's '../oath2/callback' segment.

6. We will not leverage this flow in our demonstration but note that it is very much possible to use SAML bindings between the Corporate Identity Provider and IAS. The federation works exactly as OIDC.

 

7. Next, we want to demonstrate a dynamic / Group assertion / Role Collection based user role/authorization determination. For that note that on the Azure side, we have a group called 'IntegrationDevelopers' that contains the users who must be authorized to call the IFlow / API on the Cloud Integration side. 

8. Notice how the 'groups' claim on the IAS side resolves to the value of the group from Azure.

9. Similarly, see that the target user has been assigned to the 'IntegrationSuiteDevelopers' Group in Okta.

10. Okta presents the user's 'Groups' claim to IAS that XSUAA will resolve in a later step. 

11. As a last configuration step, notice that there is a RoleCollection on the BTP side (with the 'MessagingSend' role assigned) mapped to the respective groups from the source identity providers.

 

Step 2 & 3: Initiate the client flow.

The easiest way to demonstrate a client flow is to do so in Postman which natively supports simulating an OAuth 2.0 3-legged Authorization Code grant flow. We can break down the segments of the 3-legged flow in a browser as well. I will demonstrate both of these user agents.

Summary of the steps about to be performed in this section

ObjectiveSteps
Use Postman to set up Authorization Code flow with Okta Identity Provider1-8
Use Postman to set up Authorization Code flow with Entra ID Identity Provider9
Usage of Refresh Tokens13-14
Use Browser to set up Authorization code flow with Identity Providers15-18

1. Within the 'Authorization' tab in Postman, set the 'Type' to 'OAuth 2.0' and the 'Grant type' to 'Authorization Code'.

2. Enter the values for the Callback URL, Auth URL, Access Token URL, Client ID, Client Secret from the values saved in the Step 0 block above.

3. Click on 'Get New Access Token'. Make sure to turn on the 'Console' tab at the bottom to keep track of requests and responses across the wire.

4. Postman will launch the Logon pop-up from BTP's Authorization Server. Notice that you are presented with a list of Identity Providers to log into as configured in BTP's Trust Management section. Select the one that corresponds to your IAS Tenant.
Pay attention to the GET requests in the Console tab. You will see that the request to the 'authorize' resource is being redirected to the login page.

5. The system will prompt you to present the user identifier, this will serve as an input to the 'Conditional Authentication' block set in the IAS tenant to resolve which corporate identity provider to redirect to, for the user logon challenge.

6. The system determines that the challenge should come from Okta IDP for my *.sap.com user name. Please refer to the 'Conditional Authentication' screenshot to get a summary of the determination process.
In the 'Console' section, make a note of how the callbacks are handled.

Conditional Authentication section in SAP IAS

 

7. Okta will authenticate the user and present back the 'authorization code' to IAS.

8. Finally the client will exchange the authorization code for the access token from the configured token endpoint.

9. Let us now perform steps nos. 3-8 again, but this time let us log in with our *.outlook.com user that gets authenticated and authorized from Entra ID (Azure AD).

10. Upon inspection, you will note that the access token issued by XSUAA has the 'ESBMessaging.send' scope as determined by the 'Groups' claim presented by the source IDP. You will remember that we created a mapping for this resolution in a previous step. Also, note that the system bears a refresh_token.

11. Further, if you inspect the respective JWTs issued by Okta and Entra ID, you will see that the tokens contain the claims that represent the Groups, RoleCollections, and User Identifier info.

12. Simply go ahead and 'Use Token' to load the token to make your request.

13. Using the refresh_token -> Notice that the token will expire after a set duration (based on the 'expiry' setting). As you can see in the screenshot below, Postman detects that the available token is expired. It gives an option to 'Refresh' the token. Click on this button.

14. Make a note in the Console tab that the client POSTs to the token endpoint with the available refresh_token and the refresh_token grant_type to get a fresh access token.

15. In the next screenshots, let us perform the same set of steps in a browser. We will need to frame the URL to the /oauth/authorize endpoint. The easiest way to do so would be to copy the URL from the Postman Console we referred to before. The URL is in the format :

https://<tenant-id>>/authentiation.<dc>.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/authorize?
response_type=code&client_id=<url-encoded-client-id>&redirect_uri=<url-encoded_redirect_uri>

16. Invoke the URL in a browser.

17. After the 'login' and 'authenticate' procedures, you will see that the browser is redirected to the redirect_uri location. You can copy the 'code' parameter from the URL. 

18. Go back to Postman and POST the Access Token endpoint with the grant_type set to authorization_code and the copied code and the redirect_uri. The server will respond with the access_token with the same set of attributes populated as demonstrated in Step 11.

 

Step 4: Integration Flow Reciever side propagation

Now that we have an access_token that can be presented to the Cloud Integration runtime (to a 'Sender Adapter'), let us put together a simple IFlow that can demonstrate the fact that the user's identity from the external identity provider can be propagated to 3 backend systems - a) S/4HANA Onpremse, b) SuccessFactors and c) S/4HANA Cloud via Principal Propagation and OAuth2SAMLBearer mechanisms respectively.

Here is a summary of the steps we intend to achieve:

ObjectiveSteps
Create a sample IFlow that demonstrates the user propagation sequence to 3 different types of backend systems.1-2
Invoke S4HANA Cloud backend 3 - 7
Invoke SAP SuccessFactors backend8 - 13
Invoke SAP S/4HANA Onpremise backend14 - 17

1. Let's start by putting together a simple IFlow to illustrate the user propagation flow. Since we are planning to invoke with 3 backends, the quickest way to demonstrate this would be to create a Router that has 3 branches. Each with a 'Request-Reply' step for the backend type, S/4HANA Cloud, SuccessFactors, and S/4HANA OnPremise respectively.

2. The logic we will follow is that the client passes a value in a custom header named 'target' that shall determine which of the routes is to be invoked.

3. In the property sheet of the HTTP Receiver for S/4HANA Cloud backend, notice that we've used a credential named 's4hanaCloudCredentials' with the OAuth2 SAML Bearer Assertion type.

4. I will not get into the details behind how the attributes of this Security Material have been formulated. Refer to parts of this blog post for details. The points worth mentioning here are that a) we are using the target system type SAP BTP (CF) and b) the 'userIdSource' attribute is annotated for 'email' & nameIdFormat is set to 'urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:emailAddress', thereby implying that the user identifier from our original JWT token negotiated with the corporate identity provider will serve as the user principal to be propagated.

5. Let us make a call to the IFlow URL with the access token set from step 8 described in the above section. Note that we've set the 'target' header attribute to 's4hanacloud'  so that the call gets executed in the first route. We get an HTTP 200 OK and the service document as the response and there you have it! We were able to successfully propagate the user from an external identity provider and execute a call in an S/4HANA backend with the user's context.

6. How do I prove my point that the user was indeed propagated? The next two screenshots do so. Note that on the S/4HANA side, I have a 'Business User' that bears my (that is propagated from Okta) emailID. Also, note that the HTTP Call is executed with this user context and NOT with a Communication User (technical user) attached to the Communication Arrangement.

7. Further to prove my point, I execute step no. 5, this time by presenting my *@outlook.com user (that comes from Entra ID), you see that the call fails and the error description calls out that the backend was not able to resolve the presented *.outlook.com user.

8. Let us now look at the 2nd route, the one that invokes a SuccessFactors URL. We extend the same 'OAuthSAMLBearer Assertion' type with a credential named 'SFSFUserPrincipal'. On the processing tab, you will see that I'm invoking a GET Query on the JobProfile resource.

9. In the details of the Security Material, note that we've set the attributes per SuccessFactors documentation. The User ID is set for principal propagation. Like before, we've used the same nameIdFormat as set in step 4 above, and don't forget to include the apiKey attribute as well.

10. Let us now invoke the IFlow, this time around with the header 'target' set to 'sfsf'. I get back a response from SuccessFactors with the JobProfile details.

11. Again, how do we prove that the call indeed was made in the signed-in user's context? There are many ways to establish this. A simple way I followed was to put a 'proxy' layer like API Management before the call hits the SuccessFactors backend and print out the 'Bearer token' from the 'Authorization' header.  Upon Base64 decoding the token, you will see that the token bears a 'sfPrinciple' attribute with the employee ID identifier. 

12. Look up the employee profile of the user in question in your SuccessFactors tenant and you can verify the matching employee ID and the corresponding email address.

13. Negative testing -> If I perform the call again, this time by signing in with the email address from Entra ID you should see a 401 unauthorized exception stating that the propagated user wasn't resolved. 

14. Finally, we are down to the last segment of our testing. A connection to S/4HANA On-premise. I've configured an SAP Cloud Connector and an X.509 certificate signing procedure (that is beyond the scope of this demonstration) and have dialed 'Principal Propagation' for the authentication type. 

15. Invoking the client this time around with the 'target' header set to 's4hanaonpremise'. I get back a response from the server with my service document for the invoked GWSAMPLE_BASIC OData service.

16. As a quick verification step, let us go to the 'Monitor' section in the Cloud Connector and within the 'Most Recent Requests' tab, you can see a record for the 'User' that was propagated.

17. Open the LJSTrace log file and you can hunt down a log entry that corresponds to the user subject that was propagated via the short-lived x.509 certificate.

Phew!

Summary:

It is beyond doubt that 'client credentials' and 'x.509 certificate' are the two most prominent and widely popular ways to authenticate to an Integration Flow / API artifact in SAP Integration Suite, but should you have a requirement to authenticate and authorize with the client user's identity from a corporate Identity Provider, OpenID Connect support from SAP Cloud Identity Service along with the Authorization Code grant type in SAP Integration Suite provide an excellent and out-of-box approach to get your job done.

Cheers, and more power to the  SAP Integration Suite  Community!

entry.title: Re: Option for user to select versions in waterfall chart
entry.content: Not sure why to use range filter to select version, but not using normal input control select by member?

entry.title: Re: JMS Receiver channel ping issue.
entry.content:
Hello Chandar,

So far, I haven't seen any effect of the error received with Ping Destination on JMS channels. I think you can ignore it.

Thanks.

Caner.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Peppol e-Invoices Singapore - Part 2
entry.content: @XavierT Currently no plan, you can check SAP Road Map Explorer for any changes/updates in future.

entry.title: Professional Services- Pre-Sales Resource Estimator
entry.content:
Hi Community, 

Customer has implemented Professional Services in Public Cloud and looking for scope items and/or apps that will allow them to plan and estimate project resources, roles, job level, resources, candidate rates, client rates, total days/ effort, total sell, total margin, margin percentage, other project expenses etc. at the pre-sales stage, without having to set up a project, customer BP etc. first, and without the need for additional licenses. 

Please could you advise on best approach. Currently using Excel to do this pre-sales planning and estimating. 

Thank you, 

Linne

entry.title: How to allow Manual posting to GR/IR GL Account.
entry.content:
How we can allow manual posting to GR/IR GL Account.

Business new requirement came that they wanted to allow manual posting to GR/IR GL Account one time, is it possible?

entry.title: CO60 Loading issue
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Am facing an issue while running the T-code co60, after running the T-code it is not going further after selecting PI sheet, It popping up Loading data for the whole day,

Please help me out.

Thanks In Advance

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 0
entry.content:
What is that a picture of?

Unfortunately, the hash is not correct. I can see that you did not enter you community ID. Your community ID is what is shown at the top left of your post: anumalasingh

entry.title: Re: How to allow Manual posting to GR/IR GL Account.
entry.content:
Hello,

once you remove the automatic posting check box for GR/IR Gl account, then system will allow to post manually.

Thanks

Subbareddy

entry.title: SAP Exception while calling RFC from .NET MVC Framework.
entry.content:
I'm using the .NET MVC Framework (v 4.8) to call an RFC function of SAP using RfcDestinationManager from sap.middleware.connector (sapnco.dll and sapnco_util.dll).

The website is hosted on an IIS server with a .NET CLR Version of v4.0.

The issue I'm encountering arises when I call the RFC with the parameter FunctionalLocationID to retrieve SAP notification numbers. While it operates smoothly approximately 80% of the time, occasionally it crashes within the IIS server. Interestingly, there are no exceptions in the code, nor any error traces on the SAP side. The only indication of the issue appears in the Event Viewer of the hosting server.

Code

Below, I've attached screenshots of the encountered errors for further examination.

entry.title: SAP MDK Form Cell Header reference
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I want to set enabled/disabled my FormCell header items ( button) based on some actions.

I am trying to get reference of the header using clientApi proxy, but I am not able to navigate to that level.

How to get reference to the Header of form cell container ? If it is not possible how do I get the button using name ?

cc : @bill_froelich , @Jitendra_Kansal 

Thanks,

Ram
